title: fagara occidentalis
intro: Fagara occidentalis, also known as the Wild Prickly Ash, is a deciduous shrub native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its compound called salicin. The health benefits of Fagara occidentalis include relief from pain and fever, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestive issues. Fagara occidentalis can be prepared as a herbal remedy through teas, infusions, or salves, with its bark and roots being commonly used for medicinal purposes.
